updatedBy: Codex / serialized post-tiered-merge-governance and Phase 0/1 audit
lastVerifiedCommit: 3cc9a98 after PR #264 merged; GitHub verify, docs, evals, Database contracts, and Vercel Preview Comments passed, and the rebased documentation control state passed the full local gate
maturity: Real-provider Copilot, durable two-stage Trip completion, truthful product states, anonymous-turn and trusted-IP guards, controlled-preview Human Task operations, and private evidence review are merged. Tiered merge governance and a reconciled canonical Phase 0/1 dependency graph are now active. Public fact provenance is review-pending, not merged; the product is not yet production-Redis verified, information-architecture unified, billing-ready, or production-release complete.
lastCompleted: PR #265 established risk-tiered merge authority without weakening CI, base-main, no-stacked, or serialized-handoff controls. PR #264 then reconciled the canonical dependency graph to merged Phase 0 evidence and removed stale control states. Both were Tier A governance changes, passed their real GitHub checks, and merged to main.

activeWork.title: Expose privacy-safe public POI fact provenance
activeWork.state: The base-main Tier B PR is open, clean, non-draft, and all reported GitHub checks are green. It renders only eligible source class, label, and verification date, with no raw locator, evidence, reviewer, or private metadata. It is not merged and therefore is not mainline capability.
activeWork.next: Independently review the privacy/evidence boundary and decide whether reviewed merchant evidence maps to operator_verified or requires a future schema-first source class. Merge or request changes; Codex must not self-merge this D2/Tier B PR.

activeWork.title: Unify the Web shell and mobile Copilot information architecture
activeWork.state: P0-10 and P0-11 are complete, but the acceptance requires Privacy, Terms, and disclosure routes. #75 is still blocked on an accountable operator/legal content baseline, so GitHub metadata is status:blocked and no implementation branch is active.
activeWork.owner: Operator/legal reviewer for #75; Codex after acceptance
activeWork.next: Accept and record #75's entity, privacy, affiliate, Human Help, and emergency wording baseline; implement its routes; then resume P0-12 without inventing legal promises or linking to 404 pages.

activeWork.title: Demo cost guard and rate-limit controls
activeWork.state: PRs #258 and #260 closed #246 and #247, so the anonymous-turn and trusted-IP code guards are merged. OA-012/OA-013 still gate deployed Redis evidence. #248 runtime persistence remains D2/Tier B blocked because the frozen schema cannot truthfully represent provider cached-input pricing and the router can collapse missing pricing to USD 0; #249 depends on #248.
activeWork.owner: Operator for OA-012/OA-013; architecture for #248 pricing freeze; Codex after acceptance
activeWork.next: Verify both Redis guards in Vercel without exposing secrets. Architecture must either extend the cost contract with cached-input units, pricing basis, and provenance or explicitly accept a conservative uncached ceiling. Do not write fake zero-cost rows or start #249 before that decision.

nextActions.action: Complete independent Tier B review of PR #266 and resolve the merchant-source wording mismatch in #245.
nextActions.exitCriteria: The reviewer accepts or requests changes to the public provenance projection and records whether reviewed merchant evidence maps to operator_verified or needs a separate schema-first contract.

nextActions.action: Resolve #75's D3 operator/legal content decision before resuming P0-12.
nextActions.exitCriteria: The accountable entity/controller/contact, effective date, privacy categories/processors/retention, affiliate disclosure, Human Help limits, and emergency wording are accepted and versioned without payment promises.

nextActions.action: Obtain architecture acceptance for #248 cached-input pricing, pricing snapshots, and missing-pricing behavior.
nextActions.exitCriteria: Token units, cache-hit price, pricing basis, provenance, effective dates, fallback attribution, and fail-honest behavior are frozen without treating unknown price as USD 0.

nextActions.action: Complete OA-012 and OA-013 deployed Redis verification without weakening either fail-closed guard.
nextActions.exitCriteria: Sanitized Vercel evidence shows three-success/one-registration-wall behavior and a trusted-address 429 with matching Retry-After; Redis contains only hashed identities with the documented TTLs and no secret or raw identifier.

blockers: PR #266 / #245 is Tier B review-pending. Its public projection is green but not merged; the Issue's merchant-source example does not exist in the frozen source-class contract, so architecture must accept operator_verified mapping or require a schema-first follow-up.

P0-12 is D2-blocked by #75: the required Privacy, Terms, affiliate, Human Help, and emergency routes do not exist, and their accountable operator/legal wording is not accepted. Do not invent legal copy or add dead links.

#248 runtime writer is D2/Tier B blocked until cached-input units, provider pricing snapshot provenance, pricing basis, and missing-pricing behavior are accepted; the current router can report USD 0 when prices are absent, which is not auditable cost truth.

#246 and #247 code are merged, but production Redis evidence awaits OA-012/OA-013 configuration. Until then deployed Copilot requests fail honestly when either guard is unavailable; do not claim either limit is production-verified.
#249 has its #247 trusted-IP prerequisite, but remains D2-blocked by #248 because an Ops cost summary and durable rate-limit/product events cannot be truthful before runtime persistence exists.
V2-38 #59 is D2-blocked until architecture accepts an evidence-eligibility matrix in place of the stale raw 200-page target; downstream SEO Issues #84 through #86 must not generate filler pages.

V2-59 #72 needs architecture to decide whether PR #195 plus typed 503 satisfies provider self-check or whether degraded public health belongs to P0-20; V2-61 #74 remains blocked until P0-20 freezes safety assertions.
P0-03 and P0-04 code are merged, but real Supabase production evidence still awaits OA-001 through OA-004 before full production readiness can be claimed.
Ops RBAC code is merged; real first-admin and deployed role evidence awaits OA-010.
P0-17 remains blocked by the accepted payment/legal decision gates and OA-006; no real paid service is claimed.
Phase 1/2/3 functional rollout remains trigger-gated even where tooling or schema preparation has merged.
verification: PR #264 merged at 3cc9a98 after GitHub verify, docs, evals, Database contracts, and Vercel Preview Comments passed. The rebased documentation-only control change also passed local typecheck, lint, build, test, evals, docs:check, and docs:impact after repairing its formatting failure. It reconciles dependency metadata only and does not claim new runtime behavior.

PR #265 merged at e6837b2 after GitHub verify, docs, evals, Database contracts, and Vercel Preview Comments passed, with the full local gate green. It permits tightly checked Tier A self-merge only when architecture is unavailable; Tier B remains independently reviewed, CI is unchanged, base must remain main, stacked PRs remain forbidden, and serialized handoff rules remain active.

PR #266 is open and not merged. On its current base-main head, GitHub verify, docs, evals, Database contracts, and Vercel Preview Comments are green; Supabase Preview is skipped by integration. Local full gates, focused projection tests, and explicit WebKit acceptance at 375/768/1280 passed, with no horizontal overflow or raw source locator/evidence leakage. These are review artifacts, not mainline capability evidence.

PR #260 merged at 8e07982 after verify, docs, evals, Database contracts, and Vercel Preview Comments passed. Full local typecheck, lint, build, test, evals, and documentation gates passed. Route tests proved spoofed x-forwarded-for cannot bypass Vercel's trusted-header identity and missing trust/config fails before model work. Redis 7.4 exercised the production sliding-window Lua contract through the tenth allowed request, eleventh-request 60-second block, post-window recovery, HMAC-only sorted-set key shape, and one-hour TTL; this is local protocol evidence only, not production Upstash evidence. WebKit at 375/768/1280 verified the honest 429 state and mobile scroll correction with no horizontal overflow; the HTTP response was visually intercepted and is not backend evidence.

PR #258 merged at 4026958 after verify, docs, evals, Database contracts, and both Vercel deployments passed. Full local typecheck, lint, build, test, evals, and documentation gates passed. WebKit verified three successful anonymous turns, a fourth-request 403 before model execution, a usable registration path, and no 375px overflow. Redis 7.4 exercised the production Lua reserve/complete/release contract, including concurrent capacity, idempotent completion, completed-limit behavior, hashed-key fields, and a 30-day TTL; this is local protocol evidence only, not production Upstash evidence.

PR #257 merged at a7373d4 after verify, docs, evals, Database contracts, and Vercel Preview Comments passed. It completed P0-16 with permission-bound append-only terminal-task evidence, email/phone redaction, inherited task retention/deletion, identifier-only audit, and sanitized open-gap proposals that cannot auto-publish facts. Local Postgres integration passed 10 tests; 11 pgTAP files passed 140 tests; browser evidence verified redaction, open-gap review state, no 375px overflow, and no console errors. The earlier serialized snapshot missed this already-merged PR; this reconciliation corrects that D2 observation rather than reimplementing the feature.

PR #255 merged at 9a8bd34 after verify, docs, evals, Database contracts, and both Vercel deployments passed. The CI database suite passed 17/17 after removing an invalid cross-test audit-table ordering assumption. Local Postgres verified six Human Task integration cases; browser acceptance verified queue-to-detail, note persistence, requested-to-triaged history, zero console errors, and no 375px overflow.

PR #253 merged at c036239 after verify, docs, evals, Database contracts, both Vercel deployments, and local typecheck/lint/test/build/docs/evals passed. Browser acceptance at 375/768/1280 found no horizontal overflow; the editable example-question path and honest local 503 retry state were verified.
PR #251 merged at ba3401d after verify, docs, evals, Database contracts, and Vercel Preview Comments passed. It completed owner-scoped P0-10 Web resume without creating a second assistant bubble.
PR #242 merged at 7fc8e07 after verify, docs, evals, Database contracts, and Vercel Preview Comments passed. V2-75 is closed; this tooling merge does not waive Phase 1 rollout triggers.
PR #250 merged at a688c7b after verify, docs, evals, Database contracts, and Vercel Preview Comments passed. It freezes #248 schemas only; no runtime persistence or complete cost evidence is claimed.
PRs #229, #240, and #251 completed the durable P0-10 provenance, queue/callback/retry, and Web resume sequence; parent Issue #118 is closed.
PRs #232 and #241 completed P0-13 durable Human Task intake and P0-14 transition enforcement, which PR #255 now consumes for authorized controlled-preview triage.
P0-07b production smoke previously returned typed real-provider Copilot responses. #248 remains responsible for the expanded auditable conversation, product-event, and per-attempt cost records.

GitHub metadata audit on 2026-07-21 found no open Issue carrying status:ready. #120, #248, #249, #59, #72, #74, and their downstream controls remain at explicit blockers; no trigger-gated Phase 2/3 implementation was started.

readingOrder.reason: Learn the product thesis, canonical language, relationships, and truth hierarchy.
Understand mission, controlled subsystems, interface baselines, observations, and lifecycle gates.
Understand the composite Qian, Matt-inspired documentation, and Karpathy workflow plus D0-D3 correction model.
See authority, precedence, reading order, document classes, implementation gate, verification, and handoff as one portable baseline.
Read the frozen product, commercial, architecture, roadmap, and anti-goal baseline.
Compare the frozen target with the latest evidence-based implementation audit.
Resolve the one canonical owner, dependencies, trigger gates, and historical migrations before taking any Issue.
Choose the module document that owns the assigned work and inspect its real maturity.
Load the mandatory architecture, coding, business, permission, deployment, and iteration rules.
Apply explicit assumptions, minimum sufficient design, surgical diffs, and step-to-check verification during implementation.
Check accepted decisions before proposing or implementing a direction change.
Read the relevant operational procedure before deployment, migration, AI, knowledge, or Human Task work.
Check unresolved third-party, secret, legal, DNS, deployment, and production-verification actions before claiming an external capability is live.
